title: Morgane Gonon
text:
Welcome! I am a PhD candidate in Ecological Economics at CIRED (Centre International de Recherche sur l’Environnement et le Développement), AgroParisTech, and Université Paris-Saclay.
My research lies at the intersection of ecological economics, sustainable finance, and political economy. I study how financial systems can — or fail to — support ecological transitions, with a focus on biodiversity finance, climate finance, and macro-financial constraints on sustainability.
I develop analytical frameworks connecting funding sources, implementing agents, and ecological actions (the FAE framework) to diagnose financing gaps and assess the political economy of environmental policy instruments. My work draws on institutional economics, Stock-Flow Consistent macroeconomic modelling, and mixed-methods empirical research.
My case studies span Senegal, Colombia, and France, with a particular interest in North–South financial flows, official development finance, debt-for-nature swaps, and voluntary carbon markets.
Contact: morgane.gonon [at] agroparistech.fr

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Skills
Methods: Ecological economics, institutional economics, Stock-Flow Consistent (SFC) macroeconomic modelling, econometrics, non-parametric statistics, optimal transport, multi-objective optimisation, sensitivity analysis, NLP/text analysis
Programming: R (data.table, tidyverse, Rcpp), Python, LaTeX
Languages: French (native), English (fluent)

title: Research
text: My research develops at the interface of ecological economics, sustainable finance, and political economy. I focus on financing gaps for biodiversity and climate action, the macro-financial conditions for ecological transitions, and the institutional economics of environmental finance instruments.

title: Research
text:
Peer-Reviewed Articles
Subsidies against Nature: A Multidimensional Framework with Adeline Vallet, Vincent Deschamps, et al. Ecological Economics 235, 2025 [Paper]

Selective Carbon Credits: Market Preferences and Ecosystem Restoration in Senegal with Romain Prudhomme, Mamadou Ba, et al. Ecological Economics 235, 2025 [Paper]

Chronicle of a Debt Foretold: Green Debt-for-Nature Swap Strategies with Antoine Godin, Louis Daumas, et al. International Review of Economics and Finance, 2026 — forthcoming
